patterns.title: Evidence-first diagnosis
patterns.purpose: Reduce speculative edits by grounding the change in repository evidence.
steps: Restate the requested behavior and identify the observable acceptance boundary.
Inspect the smallest relevant code path and existing tests before editing.
Run a focused visible check to reproduce or characterize the current behavior.
Record the evidence that distinguishes the likely cause from nearby alternatives.
guards: Do not infer hidden validator details.
Do not broaden the requested behavior without repository evidence.

patterns.title: Minimal behavioral patch
patterns.purpose: Change the narrowest implementation surface that satisfies the documented behavior.
steps: Preserve public contracts and unrelated behavior.
Prefer a small coherent change over repository-wide rewriting.
Keep generated, vendored, lock, and configuration files unchanged unless the task requires them.
Review the final diff for unrelated changes before completion.
guards: A historical patch is not the only valid solution.
Do not encode a task-specific answer from examples or fixtures.

patterns.title: Layered validation
patterns.purpose: Use progressively broader checks while preserving a fixed work budget.
steps: Run the narrowest visible check after the first coherent edit.
If it fails, use the visible failure to revise the causal hypothesis rather than patching the assertion text.
Run relevant type, lint, build, or regression checks when available and within budget.
Stop only after the visible checks pass or the resource budget is exhausted.
guards: Hidden validators remain scorer-side and provide no iterative feedback.
Preserve every failed check and retry in the run transcript.

patterns.title: Failure-informed retry
patterns.purpose: Turn a visible failed attempt into bounded new evidence.
steps: Classify the failure as setup, syntax, type, behavioral, regression, or resource related.
Reinspect the smallest code and state surface implicated by that failure.
Make one causally motivated revision before rerunning the focused check.
Avoid repeating an unchanged action after the same failure.
guards: Do not use network access or external production state.
Do not treat a timeout or malformed tool result as task evidence.
